entries.text:
How posting a job works
Five quick steps — Basics, Description, Compensation, Screening, and Status. You can save a draft and finish anytime; nothing goes live until you set the status to Active.
1 · Basics
Title, role, employment type, and which of your locations the role is at. This is the only step with required fields.
2 · Description
Write it yourself, or on Growth+ let the AI generator draft it from the basics — it fills the field automatically and you edit freely after.
3 · Compensation
Set a range and how it's paid. Showing a range gets you materially more applicants; you can also keep it hidden while still powering internal matching.
4 · Screening questions
Optional knockout and free-response questions. Knockouts are soft — a wrong answer flags the applicant for you, it never auto-rejects them.
5 · Status
Save as Draft to finish later, or set Active to publish. You can unpublish anytime.

AI job-description generator
Writes a first draft from your Basics and applies it straight into the description field. Edit it freely after — it's a starting point, not a lock-in.
Drafts from the title, role, and details you've already entered.
Applies directly into the editor — no separate “apply” step.
Fully editable after; rewrite or trim anything.
There's a daily usage cap to keep costs sane — you'll see a note if you hit it.

Show compensation
Listings that show a pay range get noticeably more applicants. If you hide it, the range still powers matching behind the scenes — candidates just won't see the number.

Composable compensation
Layer base, variable/bonus, and equity. Each piece can show on the public listing or stay internal — useful when the package is more than a flat salary.
Base range is the headline number candidates see.
Variable / bonus and equity can be shown or kept internal per role.
What you mark internal never renders on the public listing.

Preferred skills
Not a hard filter. Practice Fit rewards candidates who match a few of these; missing a skill never disqualifies anyone.

Requirements
The must-haves shown on the listing — license, education, experience. Pick from the role-specific suggestions or type your own; each one becomes its own line.

Screening questions
Ask knockout or free-response questions on the application. Knockouts are soft — a missed answer flags the applicant for your review, it doesn't auto-reject.
Knockout questions flag, they don't reject — you stay in control.
Free-response questions collect context without gating.
Avoid questions that touch protected characteristics; the builder nudges you off these.

Soft knockout
“Soft” means a wrong answer flags the applicant for you to review — it never auto-rejects them. You decide.

Verification requirements
Mark which credentials this role needs verified. DSO Hire is the conduit — you or a sanctioned third party do the verifying; we never assert a credential is verified ourselves.
Tells candidates what they'll be asked to furnish.
DSO Hire passes credentials through — it is not the verifier.
Verification is set by your own diligence or a sanctioned third-party service.

Candidate visibility
By default candidates see exactly where they sit in your pipeline. Turn this on only for a sensitive or executive search to show an abstracted “In review” instead.

Internal-only job
Hidden from public search and the job feed. Share the direct link with the people you want to apply — useful for confidential or internal-mobility roles.

How the pipeline works
Applicants flow through stages you can drag them between. Bulk-select to move several at once. Candidates see their stage unless you've hidden it on the job.
Stages
New, Reviewed, Interviewed, Offered, Hired, Rejected by default — rename or reorder them in Settings → Pipeline.
Move candidates
Drag a card between columns, or use the Stage selector on the card. Bulk-select to move many at once.
What the candidate sees
By default candidates see their current stage. If you turned on hidden stages for this job, they see an abstracted “In review” until Offer or Hired.
Scorecards & comments
Leave structured interview feedback and @mention teammates right on a candidate card.

Pipeline stages
Each column is a stage in your hiring flow. Rename or reorder them in Settings → Pipeline — changes apply across your jobs.

Practice Fit score
A guidance score, not a gate. It blends role, skills, schedule, and preferences — and drops any dimension the candidate left blank so sparse profiles aren't penalized.
Blends role match, skills, schedule overlap, and stated preferences.
Missing data drops from the denominator — a thin profile isn't punished.
Buckets: Strong / Good / Fair / Low. It's a sort aid, never an auto-decision.

Scorecards
Structured interview feedback attached to a candidate. Keeps interviewer notes consistent and comparable across your team.

Bulk actions
Select multiple candidates to move, reject, or archive them in one go. Candidate notifications still follow your per-action settings.

Messaging candidates
Threaded messaging with inline cards for interviews and offers, plus attachments. Replies notify the candidate by email.
One thread per candidate; rich cards embed interviews and offers inline.
Attach files directly in the thread.
The candidate gets an email when you message; they can reply in-thread.

Propose an interview
Offer times right in the thread. The candidate books one, and it lands on your connected calendar automatically.

Send an offer
Send an offer card in-thread. The candidate accepts or declines on a secure tokenized page, and the pipeline stage flips for you automatically.

Talent Pool
Search candidates who've opted into discovery. Browsing isn't contacting — you reach out deliberately, and candidate privacy settings are always respected.
Only candidates who opted into discovery appear here.
Filter by role, skills, location, and more.
Browsing a profile doesn't notify or contact anyone.

Verified vs. self-reported
Items marked verified were confirmed by your own diligence or a sanctioned third party. Everything else is self-reported by the candidate — DSO Hire never asserts verification itself.

Plans & tiers
Four plans — Solo, Growth, Scale, Enterprise. Every paying tier gets every feature in its tier; there's no feature gating beyond the plan you're on.
Solo — smaller groups getting started.
Growth — midsize, multi-location.
Scale — larger, multi-region operations.
Enterprise — the largest, most complex groups.

Annual billing
Pay annually for roughly 10% off versus monthly. Same features either way.

Practice locations
Locations power job placement, per-location analytics, and let you scope Hiring Managers to just the practices they run.

DSO affiliation
Controls whether a location publicly shows it's part of your group. Set a location private and its jobs won't link back to your corporate brand.

Email templates
Customize the 3 automatic candidate emails (apply confirmation, message-received, stage-moved) on any paid tier. Growth+ also unlocks unlimited custom templates you can send on demand from a candidate's profile.
Predefined templates (3 of them) edit the subject + body of emails the platform sends on candidate events — available on every paid tier including Solo.
Custom templates (Growth+) are reusable templates you author yourself — interview prep, offer follow-ups, no-show outreach — and send on demand from any candidate's profile.
Both surfaces use mergefields like {{candidate.first_name}} and {{job.title}}. Preview pane shows the rendered email before you save or send.

Send a custom email to one candidate
On Growth+, open any candidate's application detail and click 'Send email' next to the Pipeline stage. Pick one of your custom templates, preview the rendered version, and send.

Two-factor authentication (2FA)
Optional security upgrade — adds a 6-digit code from an authenticator app to your sign-in. Trusted devices skip the code prompt for 30 days, so it stays low-friction.
Set it up
Settings → Account → Two-factor authentication. Scan the QR code with 1Password / Authy / Apple Passwords / Google Authenticator, enter the 6-digit code, and save the 10 recovery codes somewhere safe.
Trust this device
On the next sign-in, leave 'Trust this device for 30 days' checked. You won't be prompted for the code again on that browser for 30 days unless you clear cookies, switch networks, or disable + re-enroll MFA.
Require it for your whole team
DSO owners can flip 'Require 2FA for the whole DSO' on the same settings page. Every team member is then forced to enroll at their next sign-in. Available on any paid tier.
Lost your device?
Use one of the 10 recovery codes from setup — each works once and reveals the option to re-enroll a fresh authenticator. If you've used them all, email support and we'll verify your identity to reset.

Bulk add locations from a spreadsheet
Upload a CSV or Excel file with one row per practice. We'll validate each row, insert the valid ones, and geocode them in the background. Up to 1000 rows / 5 MB per upload.
Download the sample
Locations → Bulk Import → Download sample CSV. Three example rows showing exactly the columns we expect — replace them with your own data and save.
Required vs optional columns
Required: name, city, state (2-letter code like KS or MO). Optional: address_line1, address_line2, postal_code, website. Header aliases work — 'zip' → postal_code, 'practice name' → name, 'street' → address_line1, etc.
Upload
Drag the file onto the dropzone (or click to pick), then click Import. Per-row validation surfaces inline — a deliberately broken row gives you a row number + the specific error so you can fix and re-upload just the failures.
After import
Locations show up immediately. Geocoding fans out in the background (concurrency cap of 6 to stay nice to Mapbox); map view updates within a minute. Each location respects the same Public Branding toggle as the single-add path.

Pipeline settings
Rename and reorder your hiring stages here. Changes apply across all your jobs — existing candidates keep their current stage.

Getting started
A fuller profile means better matches and more relevant roles. Import your resume to fill most of it in seconds, then review before anything saves.
Build your profile
Roles you want, skills, schedule, and preferences. The more you add, the better your matches — and you choose what's visible.
Import your resume
Upload a resume and we parse it into your profile. You review every field before it saves — nothing is applied on your behalf.
Understand Practice Fit
On each job you'll see a fit score. It's guidance to help you prioritize, never a barrier to applying.
Apply with control
You see exactly where you stand in each employer's pipeline, and you decide who can discover your profile.

Your Practice Fit score
How well a role lines up with your profile — roles, skills, schedule, preferences. It's guidance to help you prioritize, not a gate. You can apply to anything.
Higher means a closer match to what you've told us you want.
Some jobs show no score when there isn't enough overlap data — that's not a no.
It never blocks you from applying.

Resume import
Upload a resume and we pull out your experience, skills, and education. You review everything before it saves — nothing applies on your behalf.

Profile visibility & data
You control who can see your profile and can hide it from employer discovery anytime. You can also export or delete your data from settings.
Toggle whether employers can discover you in the Talent Pool.
Hidden profiles still let you apply directly to jobs.
Export or delete your data anytime from Settings → Data.

Your applications
Track every role you've applied to and where it stands. The stage you see reflects what the employer has shared for that job.

Credentials
Adding licenses and certifications strengthens your matches. Items show as self-reported until an employer or third party verifies them — we don't assert verification for you.
